Video.summary: For Dale and Matt sometimes the dotted line to treasure splits and heads in opposite directions. In this edition of What’s in the Barn, time is thrust to the forefront of this multi-state, long distance, north, south search. Timeless and priceless 110 year old bikes, antique cars, airplanes, and the infamous Egberts 1930 neon Harley-Davidson sign that coined the phrase “Ride a Winner” are high on this father and son’s list of “must haves”. The clock ticks, both literally and figuratively, on one rare time piece and two old-timer’s cars, bikes, and automobiles.
Video.title: Time is Not On Their Side

Image.alt: Time is Not On Their Side
Time is Not On Their Side
Time is Not On Their Side

Video.summary: Promoters of the prestigious Amelia Island Concours have called on Dale to develop a show and field bikes for an “Orphan Bike” show. They have defined orphan bike as extremely rare, that aren't a Harley nor Indian machines.
Video.title: Orphan Bikes

Video.summary: The RM Auction Company has worked with Dale before as a buyer, seller, historian, and caretaker at the prestigious Lee Hartung auction. But this event is no last minute rush to define one man’s treasures. This is a well calculated, time consuming, multi-media, and multi motorcycle and prized art deal.
Video.title: Second Time's a Charm

Video.summary: Home Depot Senior Vice President, Gordon Erickson, is a huge fan of Wheels Through Time. He is also a huge supporter of the Wounded Warrior program. For years the hardware giant has been donating goods and services to help returning servicemen with their fix-it-up projects. Now they would like to go one step further and build a WWII military bike to raise awareness and funds. Only problem is where to get one? Enter Dale and an exhaustive, time sensitive, cross country search for a rare military machine.
Video.title: Wounded Warrior Project

Video.summary: An family farm in the mid-west contains houses, barns, outbuildings, trailers, grain silos and buses full of old stuff and perhaps the biggest stash of undiscovered old motorcycles in the country. The collection was put together by their unnamed father. Seller agrees to have Dale broker rare Excelsior for high-end client. Dale has the buyer, but will he outbid his own client for the exclusive find?
Video.title: Diamond in Dodge City

Video.summary: Matt is out for revenge. Last year Dale beat him badly at the Annual Vintage bike race in Barber, Alabama. But this year there is a whole new tale to tell. With just days until the race - Dale is secretly struggling to get the bike he beat Matt with last year up and working. Matt struggles just to locate a 100-year old bike. Matt's prayers are answered thanks to an old Catholic Priest he located who has possession of a bike. The priest might let Matt have the bike to race - but there are conditions. The toughest one: Don't tell Dale you have it. Can Matt afford to buy the bike and get it running in time for the race without Dale seeing it? Will Dale even get his broken bike started? And if they ever get to race, will Matt exact his revenge?
Video.title: Don't Tell Dale

Video.summary: Built in the mid-60’s by eccentric engineer “Wild Bill” Gelbke, the Road Dog was, and still is, the largest motorcycle ever built. It utilized aircraft tubing, Corvette disc brakes, and a Chevy truck differential to keep it stable. The one-off creation disappeared when Gelbke did in 1978. Outflanked once when the bike was spotted and sold to a private collector in 1995, Dale has vowed to locate the machine and make Wheels Through Time its final resting place.
Video.title: Biker Lore

Video.summary: Big Time Motorcycle Auction
Video.title: Vegas Payday

Video.summary: “12hrs. & Running”: Dale turns a surprise 60th birthday party into a challenge to his guests to start 120 of the rarest motorcycles in the world.
Video.title: 12 Hours and Running

Video.summary: A cross-country race to lay claim to some of the world’s most sought after works specials.
Video.title: Hidden Hillclimbers

Video.summary: A vague tip leads Matt and Dale into a WWII era military warehouse and beyond.
Video.title: Peashooter

Video.summary: Caffeine & Octane goes Turbo in the Twin Cities; Ferrari Drifter Federico Sceriffo finds a home in Atlanta.
Video.title: Twin City Turbos

Video.summary: Jaguar F-Type Project 7 crosses the Pond.
Video.title: 470MPH with Custom King George Poteet

Video.summary: Mustang Mayhem at the big show.
Video.title: Art & Oddities at Old Car City, USA

Image.alt: Art & Oddities at Old Car City, USA
Art & Oddities at Old Car City, USA
Art & Oddities at Old Car City, USA

Video.summary: Red carpet receptions for a Koenigsegg Regera.
Video.title: The Unsung Hero in the Movie Ford v. Ferrari

Image.alt: The Unsung Hero in the Movie Ford v. Ferrari
The Unsung Hero in the Movie Ford v. Ferrari
The Unsung Hero in the Movie Ford v. Ferrari

Video.summary: Covert cars that slipped into the U.S.
Video.title: Hollywood's Cars Come to Life at Picture Car Productions

Image.alt: Hollywood's Cars Come to Life at Picture Car Productions
Hollywood's Cars Come to Life at Picture Car Productions
Hollywood's Cars Come to Life at Picture Car Productions

Video.summary: "Go Fast Girl" Katherne Legge's Model A Racer.
Video.title: Rickey Gadson Stares Down a Steep Learning Curve at Rd. Atl

Image.alt: Rickey Gadson Stares Down a Steep Learning Curve at Rd. Atl
Rickey Gadson Stares Down a Steep Learning Curve at Rd. Atl
Rickey Gadson Stares Down a Steep Learning Curve at Rd. Atl

Video.summary: Mecum Auction's push for a $15,000,000 sale.
Video.title: A Sneak Peek at a Premier, Private Collection

Image.alt: A Sneak Peek at a Premier, Private Collection
A Sneak Peek at a Premier, Private Collection
A Sneak Peek at a Premier, Private Collection

Video.summary: Demetrio Montalvo likes American music, American food, and especially American cars. Only one problem - Demetrio lives in Cuba. A Visa, bestowed to him from some American friends in high places, lets him travel to the States to search for tough to find automotive parts and attend his favorite car show. Showing up to Caffeine & Octane in a cool caddy is the easy part. Picking parts to take back home presents a unique set of challenges for the Cuban car builder; Formula Drift racer Federico Sceriffo brings the first ever Formula Drift Ferrari to C&O.
Video.title: Viva Cuba!

Video.summary: Porsche collector, customizer, and Urban Outlaw, Magnus Walker is a world-wide rock star to fans of the German car company. So, when Caffeine & Octane producers invited him to travel cross country to tell the story of a hidden Porsche collection tangled in a North Georgia Vineyard, they were surprised when he signed on. No sour grapes here, what Walker found at the Wolf Mountain Winery will impress even those with the most refined palette for Porsches.
Video.title: Magnus Walker's Secret Porsche Collection

Video.summary: A Hall of Famer?s search for an Exotic Car that fits; a blind man?s ?touchy feely? approach to seeing great cars.
Video.title: Chipper Jones' International Car Show

Video.summary: Back to the future with a Majestic build from Fuller Moto that showcases 3-D printed parts, an electric motor, and hub-centric steering.
Video.title: Fuller's "2029" Majestic Build

Video.summary: Horsepower and million-dollar sticker shock, from two and four wheels, dominate this edition of C & O.
Video.title: The Fastest Man on Two Wheels

Image.alt: The Fastest Man on Two Wheels
The Fastest Man on Two Wheels
The Fastest Man on Two Wheels

Video.summary: A need for speed in a Sleepy Georgia town has engineers pushing the boundaries to exceed 3000 horsepower.
Video.title: Power Play: Proline's Search for 3000+ HP

Image.alt: Power Play: Proline's Search for 3000+ HP
Power Play: Proline's Search for 3000+ HP
Power Play: Proline's Search for 3000+ HP

Video.summary: Turn of the century cars?with wooden wheels?at 85mph?oh my!
Video.title: Turn of the Century Cars

Video.summary: Urban Outlaw and designer Magnus Walker weighs in on his beloved Porsche and the legend of Hot Wheels.
Video.title: Hot Wheel's & an Urban Outlaw

Video.summary: Tracing the origins and solving the mystery of a controversial all-aluminum bodied 512 BBI Boxer Ferrari.
Video.title: Proving Provenance on a One-Off Ferrari

Image.alt: Proving Provenance on a One-Off Ferrari
Proving Provenance on a One-Off Ferrari
Proving Provenance on a One-Off Ferrari

Video.summary: Nick Rutter shows you the most helpful and handy car care accessories
Video.title: Car Care Accessories

Video.summary: Nick Rutter shows you the how to clean and coat your matte finish
Video.title: Matte Finishes

Video.summary: Big trucks need love to. So, we turn our attention to a GMC 5500 that needs maintenance before its next trip.
Video.title: Medium Duty Maintenance

Video.summary: Bruno and Matt see what they can do to improve the handling and sway on a 2001 GMC. Plus, they setup a very Cool Ram Runner Project. You won’t want to miss it!
Video.title: Can you Handle This?

Video.summary: Ram Runner! It’s Dodge’s version of the Ford Raptor and we’ll show it you in action on Truck U.
Video.title: Ram Runner

Video.summary: We go Head to Head with two of Jeeps most talked about engines. The solid 4.0L versus the 3.8L.
Video.title: Head to Head

Video.summary: You’ve Seen Matt and Bruno’s skills in the garage; now check out their skills on the job site. We’ll take one worker’s truck and make the repairs by quitting time.
Video.title: Construction Work Truck

Video.summary: Time to get the toys out of the garage. The weather has changed, and we want to make sure our RV, boat, and UTV are ready to roll.
Video.title: Time for Toys

Video.summary: Matt and Bruno go to work on a 1986 Blazer that needs some TLC.
Video.title: Blazer TLC

Video.summary: Any shop can misdiagnose a problem. All that means is more trips back to the shop for you. Today Bruno and Matt take a look at some of the most commonly misdiagnosed problems and help you fix them for good.
Video.title: Misdiagnosis

Video.summary: In the early 1900s, race tracks built of wood sprang up across the country drawing crowds of 50,000 and more. Ray and Xtreme sports superstar Travis Pastrana break down the Battle on the Boards.
Video.title: BOARD TRACK BATTLES

Video.summary: Ray finds an old T-Bucket that made a 133 mph pass at the Bonneville Salt Flats in 1951. The team brings the Woody Lee T back to life and attempts to match its original speed.
Video.title: MODEL T

Video.summary: Ray finds a Gurney Eagle in a garage on Long Island. He wants to put the coveted machine back on the track at Indy to honor Dan Gurney. After intense restoration and scouring the globe for a Gurney-Westlake engine, the Eagle soars again.
Video.title: DAN GURNEY

Video.summary: Ray uncovers the infamous “Brawner Hawk” – from the Dean Van Lines team – that became the breakout car in the career of Mario Andretti. Ray enlists HOF mechanic Jim McGee to restore the vehicle and, along the way, discovers a replica with many of the car’s original parts. After an award winning stop at Concours Amelia Island, the car heads to the 100th running of Indy 500 where Mario Andretti himself gets behind the wheel.
Video.title: VINTAGE RACING

Video.summary: Dale Earnhardt Jr. wants Ray to help restore an El Camino, except add on a Laguna front end. It’s not a typical restoration, but it’s certainly a NASCAR performance-worthy restoration. Ray and the team build the car and then, in a bit of gamesmanship, challenge Kyle Tucker, owner of Detroit Speed, to a series of races against his award winning Camaro. But as always, Ray has an ace up his sleeve in Al Unser, Jr. as his driver.
Video.title: TWO JUNIORS

Video.summary: Follow Jeff and Kristen Patton in the wild adventures of their towing service in the scenic and sometimes dangerous Pigeon Forge TN. This episode Jeff helps a semi driver get out of a tight turn, move a sly cone manipulator, and get a couple people off a ledge. All in a days work!
Video.title: Welcome to Gatlinburg

Video.summary: Jeff and Kristen Patton are back. This episode takes the crew to capture a stolen car and rescue a ditched car. All in a days work!
Video.title: Towventure!

Video.summary: Jeff and Kristen meet an irate young man parked illegally then swing over to get a mom out of a predicament. All in a days work!
Video.title: Towrrific!

Video.summary: Jeff and Kristen work a food truck out of a windy road at a major theme park then on to a fifth wheel that is eating dirt. All in a days work!
Video.title: Theme Parks and Trailers!

Video.summary: Jeff and Kristen have their hands full with some disgruntled parkers and a creative landing by a pickup needs a little help getting off a ledge.
Video.title: Creative and Disgruntled Parking

Video.summary: Jeff and Kristen have a day with a few folks after taking their cars to impound.
Video.title: Spicy Customers
